title: Mexican-Sour Cream (Crema Mexicana)

ingredients.title: Ingredients (Yields ~2 cups)
items: 2 cups (480 ml) heavy cream (minimum 36% fat, ultra-pasteurized if storing longer)
2 Tbsp (30 g) sour cream or buttermilk (as starter culture)
½ tsp fresh lime juice (optional, for brightness)
½ tsp fine sea salt (to taste)

instruction.title: Step 1 – Culture the Cream
steps: In a sterilized glass bowl or container, combine the heavy cream with the sour cream (or buttermilk).
Stir gently with a sterilized spoon or silicone spatula until fully blended.
Cover the container loosely with a clean kitchen towel or plastic wrap (allowing airflow for fermentation).

instruction.title: Step 2 – Fermentation
steps: Leave the mixture at room temperature (70–75°F / 21–24°C) for 12–24 hours, depending on desired thickness and tang.
Once it thickens and has a pleasant, tangy aroma, whisk in lime juice (if using) and salt to taste.

instruction.title: Step 3 – Rest & Refrigerate
steps: Transfer to a clean airtight container.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours before service to stabilize texture and flavor.

instruction.title: Serving Tips
steps: Serve all three in separate bowls or arrange nicely on a platter.
Add warm corn tortillas or tortilla chips for dipping.
Garnish guacamole with a sprinkle of cilantro or diced tomato.
Use salsa to add spice and brightness; sour cream cools and balances.

chefTips: Consistency Control: For a thinner crema (for drizzling), whisk in 1–2 Tbsp whole milk before chilling. For a thicker crema (for dolloping), let it ferment the full 24 hours.
Flavor Variation: Infuse with roasted garlic purée, chipotle, or fresh cilantro after fermentation for signature restaurant touches.
Drizzle over tacos, enchiladas, and sopes
Balance heat in salsas and moles
Enhance seafood dishes like ceviche or pescado zarandeado
Elevate desserts with tropical fruits (mango, guava, pineapple)
